Add versioned schema copy step to release-next-version skill (#83)

## Summary

Update the `release-next-version` skill to copy and stamp the versioned
JSON schema as part of the release process.

### New Step 6 — Copy and stamp the JSON schema

Between updating the csproj version (§5) and committing (§7), a new
step:

1. Creates `schema/v<version>/` directory
2. Copies `schema/cnctConfig.vnext.json` →
`schema/v<version>/cnctConfig.json`
3. Patches the `"id"` field from the develop URL to the canonical
versioned URI:

`https://raw.githubusercontent.com/bgold09/cnct-net/main/schema/v<version>/cnctConfig.json`

The commit step is also updated to stage all three files
(`CHANGELOG.md`, `.csproj`, and the new schema file), and a checklist
item is added.

feat(ai-client): smart multi-provider routing with Vertex AI + Groq

Provider routing by application type:
  Accuracy-critical (tilt-detection, moderation, survey-matching,
    recommendations, onboarding) -> Vertex AI -> Groq -> Ollama -> Mock
  Speed/cost-critical (nl-commands, support, card-generation)
    -> Groq -> Ollama -> Vertex AI -> Mock

Vertex AI:
  - Hits actual Vertex AI endpoint (aiplatform.googleapis.com) to use GCP credits
  - Service account JWT auth via Node.js crypto.subtle (no GCP SDK needed)
  - Access token cached 55 min to avoid repeated token exchanges
  - Configured via GOOGLE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_JSON + GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT

Groq:
  - llama-3.1-8b-instant for nl-commands/support (lowest latency)
  - llama-3.3-70b-versatile for card-generation (quality/cost balance)
  - Free tier covers typical volume

Provider selection:
  - isProviderConfigured() gates each provider on env vars
  - Auto-fallback: if primary errors, silently tries next in chain
  - AI_PROVIDER env var forces override of routing for all tasks
  - source field in AIResponse tells you which provider answered

commit.message:
Reject small-order Curve25519 public keys in ECDH (#1091)

Add all-zeros shared secret check to Curve25519Secret::diffie_hellman(),
returning CryptoError::SmallOrderPublicKey. Also add a direct check in the
overlay auth handshake path (auth.rs:420) since it uses x25519-dalek directly.
This matches stellar-core's crypto_scalarmult() return-code check in
Curve25519.cpp:60-63, which rejects small-order points via libsodium.

commit.message:
Fix emoji aspect ratio: use char_aspect=1.0 for emoji charset

Emoji are wide characters occupying 2 terminal columns. The number of
emoji per row was already halved (out_width // 2), but char_aspect=2.0
was still applied when deriving row count, causing output to be half as
tall as expected. Emoji cells are approximately square (2 cols × 2 rows),
so char_aspect=1.0 is correct.

commit.message:
fix: replace ${#VAR} with wc -c to avoid Nunjucks comment parse error

Bash string-length syntax ${#VARNAME} contains the sequence {# which
Nunjucks interprets as the start of a comment block. Without a closing
#} it throws "expected end of comment, got end of file" and the
scaffolder fails before generating any files.

Replace with $(echo -n "$VAR" | wc -c) which is equivalent but
contains no Nunjucks-special character sequences.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

matching stellar-core's behavior where SOCI exceptions during ledger close
propagate uncaught and crash the process. The previous behavior silently
advanced in-memory state while the database rolled back, creating a split-brain

commit.message:
Support POSIX character classes via \p{...} property syntax (#116)

Wire up the 13 JDK POSIX character classes (Lower, Upper, ASCII, Alpha,
Digit, Alnum, Punct, Graph, Print, Blank, Cntrl, XDigit, Space) so they
are recognized by the \p{...} and \P{...} property escape syntax.

The POSIX range tables already existed in UnicodeTables (for the
[[:lower:]] bracket syntax) but were not reachable from the \p{Lower}
code path. Add a POSIX_PROPERTY_GROUPS map keyed by the JDK property
names and look it up in Parser.lookupUnicodeGroup().

Add comprehensive tests (PosixPropertyClassTest) covering:
- Cross-check against JDK for representative inputs on all 13 classes
- Exhaustive ASCII scan (all 128 code points) for every class
- Negation via \P{...}
- Usage inside character classes and compound patterns

Part of #112

Implement UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS flag and add flag test coverage (#115)

Fix UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS (Pattern flag 256) which was silently broken:
the flag mapped to ParseFlags.UNICODE_GROUPS which is already in the
LIKE_PERL baseline, making it a complete no-op. Now \d, \w, \s, and \b
use proper Unicode definitions when the flag is set.

Implementation:
- Add ParseFlags.UNICODE_CHAR_CLASS (bit 12) separate from UNICODE_GROUPS
- Add Unicode Perl group tables to UnicodeTables: unicodeDigit() (Nd),
  unicodeSpace() (White_Space property), unicodeWord() (L+M+Nd+Nl+Pc+Join_Control)
- Update Parser.maybeParsePerlCCEscape() to use Unicode tables when flag set
- Add EmptyOp.UNICODE_WORD_BOUNDARY and UNICODE_NON_WORD_BOUNDARY flags
- Update Compiler to emit Unicode word boundary ops
- Update NFA, DFA, BitState, and OnePass engines for Unicode word boundaries
- DFA: add FLAG_LAST_UNICODE_WORD state flag, widen emptyFlags mask to 9 bits,
  handle Unicode word boundary in deferred assertion expansion

Add comprehensive test coverage for under-tested Pattern flags:
- LiteralFlagTest: 20 tests for LITERAL flag behavior
- UnicodeCaseTest: 14 tests for UNICODE_CASE (documents SafeRE divergence)
- UnicodeCharClassTest: 28 tests for UNICODE_CHARACTER_CLASS (\d, \s, \w, \b)
- Update EmptyOpTest and ParseFlagsTest for new flag bits

Also update AGENTS.md: file issues for bugs found during other work.

Refs #108

commit.message:
🐛 Fix flaky Rakefile spec caused by project_root resolving wrong dir

The 'writes Rakefile from example' spec runs in a tmpdir with a
minimal gemspec, but configure_template_tokens! resolved project_root
via Rake.application.original_dir — pointing at the real kettle-jem
repo root. Depending on spec ordering, Gem::Specification.load could
fail on the real gemspec due to stale loader state, causing
'Gem name could not be derived' errors (~60% failure rate).

Fix: set @@project_root_override to the tmpdir so gemspec_metadata
finds the test's minimal gemspec, matching the approach already used
by the neighboring error-mode spec.

Add -o HostKeyAlgorithms=+ssh-rsa to all SSH/SCP commands to support
legacy iOS devices that only offer ssh-rsa and ssh-dss key types.
Modern SSH clients have disabled these by default for security, but
legacy jailbroken iOS devices require this support.

older iOS devices over SSH/SCP.

﻿network: fix zombie kick not closing TCP connection

After kick_peer() the registry drops its clone of writer_tx, but
PeerConnection still holds its own clone. Since mpsc::UnboundedSender
only closes when ALL clones are dropped, the I/O bridge task stays alive,
the TCP socket stays open, and the zombie's message loop keeps running --
continuing to send GetBlocks requests that spawn spawn_blocking tasks and
contend for locks, causing RPC stalls.

Fix: return Err("DISCONNECT: zombie peer kicked") immediately after
kick_peer(). run_message_loop_unified already breaks on "DISCONNECT:"
errors, which drops PeerConnection (and its writer_tx clone), closing the
channel, exiting the I/O bridge task, and sending TCP FIN.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: quote parameter expansions in env blocks to handle YAML special chars

or corrupted string reaching the bash script.

value is always parsed as a plain string regardless of its content.

Bump SkiaSharp to Skia milestone 132 (#3560)

Bump SkiaSharp to Skia milestone 132 (#3560)

Context: https://github.com/mono/skia/pull/171
Changes: https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/pull/3560/files
AI review report: https://gistcdn.githack.com/mattleibow/b1c57a78d86cc5dec3fff1182de48f01/raw/295bb903e36917d3faf80f74ba4d3ab06adcb6ec/171.html

Update SkiaSharp from Skia m119 to m132 — the managed-side companion
to the native Skia bump merged in mono/skia#171. This moves the
assembly version from 3.119.x to 3.132.0, the native soname from
119.0.0 to 132.0.0, and all NuGet package versions from 3.119.4 to
3.132.0.

~~ Why take this bump ~~

Between m119 and m132, upstream Skia gained improvements that flow
directly through to SkiaSharp users: more consistent EXIF/orientation
handling in image codecs, HDR and wide-gamut pipeline improvements,
newer color-aware filters and drop-shadows, and accumulated
raster/perlin/filter correctness fixes. This also unblocks exposing
newer Skia APIs in future releases.

~~ New API surface ~~

SKColorType gains two values for upstream HDR color types:

  * Bgra10101010XR = 25
  * RgbF16F16F16x = 26

These are wired through EnumMappings, the bytes-per-pixel and
shift-per-pixel tables in Definitions.cs, the alpha-type validation
logic, and the GL sized-format mapping in GRDefinitions.cs.

~~ Struct ABI changes ~~

  * SKJpegEncoderOptions: now includes fOrigin / fHasOrigin fields
    matching the upstream EXIF orientation support added in m132.
  * GRVkBackendContextNative and GrVkYcbcrConversionInfo: updated
    to match upstream Vulkan shape changes.

~~ Generated bindings ~~

SkiaApi.generated.cs was regenerated to match the updated C API
(174 lines changed). This includes the new color type enum values,
the Vulkan struct layout changes, and adjusted P/Invoke signatures.

~~ Native build scripts ~~

Removed skia_use_sfntly=false from all 8 platform build.cake files
(Android, iOS, Linux, macOS, Tizen, tvOS, WASM, Windows). sfntly was
removed from upstream Skia between m119 and m132; the flag is now
unrecognized.

Added GIT_SYNC_DEPS_SKIP_EMSDK=1 environment variable passthrough
in native-shared.cake's RunPython helper so that git-sync-deps does
not attempt to activate emsdk during non-WASM builds.

~~ Submodule and branch tracking ~~

  * externals/skia now points to e2260f39c4 on the skiasharp branch
    (post mono/skia#171 merge).
  * .gitmodules branch updated from xamarin-mobile-bindings to
    skiasharp, reflecting the consolidated branch name used by
    the mono/skia fork going forward.

~~ Test updates ~~

  * New m132 color types added to EnumMappingsTest and
    ToGlSizedFormat unknown-format assertions.
  * SKImage: updated refcount expectation after FromEncodedData
    (3 → 2, matching m132 internal refcount change).
  * SKColorSpace: switched to Assert.InRange for non-deterministic
    refcounts from peek.Info.
  * SKPaint/SKFont: handle woff2 typefaces returning non-null with
    empty FamilyName under m132.
  * SKBitmap: skip Bgra10101010Xr in copy tests (unsupported pixel
    color readback on current test platforms).
  * Gray8: corrected luminance values to 0x35/0x23 matching actual
    m132 HIGHP raster pipeline output.
  * GRContext: added null-check guard for Vulkan backend test.

~~ Other changes ~~

  * LoongArch Linux build mapping: loongarch64 → loong64 to match
    Skia GN's expected target_cpu.
  * VERSIONS.txt, azure-templates-variables.yml, and
    SkiaSharp.Build.props updated for the 3.132.0 version line.

Original community contribution by @ramezgerges, with follow-up
fixes by @mattleibow.

fix: Beta bug fixes and version bump to 0.1.1-beta (#51)

* Fix 8 bugs identified during beta bug hunt

Critical:
- Fix IndexPathUtils.exists()/remove() using wrong base path (storagePath
  vs contextUser.storagePath) — remove() was deleting the wrong directory,
  leaving index data orphaned
- Fix staging table crash-and-retry corruption by deduplicating on filename
  before Delta MERGE in consolidateMainStaging()

High:
- Fix AriadneJoinRule temporal dedup using join columns instead of temporal
  columns — caused different results depending on which optimizer path fired
- Fix IndexLock.readLock() wrapping Gson null result in Some() — change
  Some(gson.fromJson(...)) to Option(gson.fromJson(...)) to prevent NPE

Medium:
- Add null guard for indexes field in IndexMetadata.apply() migration chain
- Add positive-value validation for lockTimeout and lockRetryInterval
- Add positive-value validation for indexRepartitionCount
- Filter null filenames in AriadneScan file collection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix H2, H5, M9: exploded field collision, joinDf validation, remove() resilience

H2: Exploded field indexes from the same array column (e.g., users.id as
user_id and users.name as user_name) caused AMBIGUOUS_REFERENCE errors
during build. Storage columns now use as_column names instead of
array_column names, giving each field a unique Delta column. Updated
storageColumns, buildExplodedFieldIndexes, autoBloomEligibleColumns,
mapJoinColumnsToStorage, and analyzeFiles accordingly. The locateFiles
Map API now uses as_column names (e.g., 'user_id') instead of
array_column names (e.g., 'users').

H5: joinDf silently returned empty results when all join columns existed
in the schema but had no indexes. Now throws IllegalArgumentException
with a clear message listing the unindexed columns.

M9: IndexPathUtils.remove() threw FileListNotFoundException when the
file list didn't exist, preventing deletion of the storage directory.
Now catches exceptions from FileList.remove() and proceeds with
directory deletion.

* feat: auto-migrate pre-0.1.1 exploded field column names

Old indexes stored exploded field values under array_column names (e.g.,
'users'). Since 0.1.1, they are stored under as_column names (e.g.,
'user_id'). This migration detects old-style Delta columns on first
index read and rewrites the table with renamed columns. Large index
directories are also renamed.

The migration is idempotent and fast when not needed (single schema
check with a boolean flag). New indexes are unaffected.

Routing: Fixes GetOverlappingRanges CPU overhead from repeated JSON deserialization (#5748)

> 🤖 This PR was generated with the help of GitHub Copilot CLI agent.
---
## Motivation

Fixes #5747

`CollectionRoutingMap.GetOverlappingRanges` was spending excessive CPU
time in `JsonSerializable.GetValue<T>` due to repeated access of
`PartitionKeyRange.MinInclusive`. Each property access on
`PartitionKeyRange` triggers `JToken.ToObject<T>()` because the base
class `JsonSerializable` deserializes on every call with no caching.

A customer-reported PerfView trace showed **~14.8 seconds exclusive
CPU** in `GetOverlappingRanges` → `GetValue` out of a 21-minute trace
window.

## Changes

**`CollectionRoutingMap.cs`**:
Replaced `this.OrderedPartitionKeyRanges[i].MinInclusive` (triggers JSON
deserialization on every access) with `this.orderedRanges[i].Min`
(already-cached string materialized during construction).

**`CollectionRoutingMapBenchmark.cs`** (new):
Added BenchmarkDotNet benchmark for `GetOverlappingRanges` with
10/100/1000 partition counts.

## Benchmark Results

### Before (baseline on master)

| Method | PartitionCount | Mean | Allocated |
|--------|---------------|------|-----------|
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 10 | 1,115 ns | 288 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 10 | 1,221 ns | 256 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 100 | 14,331 ns | 1,328 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 100 | 24,717 ns | 2,368 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 1000 | 218,005 ns | 8,640 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 1000 | 346,477 ns | 16,848 B |

### After (this PR)

| Method | PartitionCount | Mean | Allocated |
|--------|---------------|------|-----------|
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 10 | 687 ns | 288 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 10 | 809 ns | 256 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 100 | 10,746 ns | 1,328 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 100 | 19,285 ns | 2,368 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_SingleRange | 1000 | 173,112 ns | 8,640 B |
| GetOverlappingRanges_MultipleRanges | 1000 | 288,649 ns | 16,848 B |

### Improvement Summary

| Method | 10 partitions | 100 partitions | 1000 partitions |
|--------|--------------|----------------|-----------------|
| **SingleRange** | **1.6x faster** | **1.3x faster** | **1.3x faster**
|
| **MultipleRanges** | **1.5x faster** | **1.3x faster** | **1.2x
faster** |

> **Note:** Further optimization by replacing the per-call
`SortedList<string, PartitionKeyRange>` allocation with index-based
dedup yields an additional **~13x improvement** (17x total vs baseline).
This is tracked as a follow-up since the partition ranges are contiguous
and sorted by construction, making the `SortedList` dedup unnecessary.

## Testing

- All 8 existing `CollectionRoutingMapTest` unit tests pass (default +
preview builds)
- New `CollectionRoutingMapBenchmark` validates performance
characteristics

Massively improved Westeros overworld geography

Completely redrawn 160x80 map with accurate Westeros features:

Coastlines & Bays:
- The Bite: large NE bay cutting into The North coast (rows 17-33)
- Ironman's Bay: inlet on west coast (rows 47-63, cols 42-56)
- Blackwater Bay: large bay near King's Landing (rows 62-70)
- All land edges have proper coast tiles

Terrain improvements:
- Beyond the Wall: four distinct Haunted Forest patches in the snow
- The North: Wolfswood (heavy west forest), Frostfangs NW mountains,
  Barrowlands (SW plains), central plains, The Bite bay on NE coast
- The Neck: narrow swamp corridor, ocean on both sides
- Westerlands: mountains (Casterly Rock cliffs) east of Ironman's Bay
- Iron Islands: three island groups inside Ironman's Bay
  (Pyke/Old Wyk, Harlaw, Great Wyk)
- Riverlands: heavy forest with fertile plains along the Trident
- The Vale: mountains with small coastal plains (Gulltown)
- Crownlands: plains with Blackwater Bay indentation
- Dragonstone: small island in Blackwater Bay
- The Reach: forest-heavy with fertile plains (Mander valley)
- Stormlands: plains with coastal mountains
- Red Mountains: full barrier with Prince's Pass + Boneway gaps
- Dorne: arid plains south of Red Mountains

Updated coordinates:
- Castle Black: row 14 (south of The Wall), col 90
- Winterfell: row 27 (central North plains), col 90
- White Harbor: row 26, col 115 (coast of The Bite)
- Moat Cailin: row 36 (in The Neck swamp), col 80
- Riverrun/Harrenhal/Eyrie/Casterly Rock repositioned
- King's Landing: north shore of Blackwater Bay (100, 60)
- Dragonstone: island in Blackwater Bay (108, 65)
- Ships now at (23,42) and (38,42)
- Player start moved to (11,42) - island plains

repository.name: hypno-kinklist
repository.description: A modern React/TypeScript implementation of the original Kinklist application, with an improved codebase and enhanced functionality.

commit.message:
fix: Corrigir BCrypt API, Program.cs, criar migrations e implementar testes unitários

- Ajustar UserService.cs para usar BCrypt.Net.BCrypt.HashPassword() e BCrypt.Net.BCrypt.Verify()
- Corrigir Program.cs: WebApplication.CreateBuilder() e configuração de JWT
- Atualizar projetos para net9.0 (compatibilidade com dotnet-ef)
- Criar migrations EF Core: InitialCreate (tabelas User e Tarefa)
- Implementar 16 testes unitários usando xUnit e Moq:
  - UserServiceTests: register (válido, email duplicado, email inválido, senha curta), login (válido, email inválido, senha errada)
  - TarefaServiceTests: create, get, getAll, update, delete com casos de sucesso e erro
- Todos os testes passando (16/16)
